findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule.lean:45-46` defines `class Comodule ... [CommSemiring R] ... [AddCommMonoid M]`, while `ComoduleCat.lean:42,46` uses `[CommRing R]` and `extends ModuleCat`; `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Semi.lean:46,55` provides `SemimoduleCat` for `[Semiring R]` with `[AddCommMonoid carrier]`.
findings.fix: Define the basic bundled category over `[CommSemiring R]` by extending `SemimoduleCat R` and provide `forget₂` to `SemimoduleCat`; add a ring/`ModuleCat` specialization only as a derived interface if later needed.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat` requires `[CommRing R]` and extends `ModuleCat`, so every object has an `AddCommGroup`, but the underlying comodule API is defined for `[CommSemiring R]` and `[AddCommMonoid M]`.

summary: The bundled category is still specialized below the natural level of the existing comodule API. The basic category should preserve the semiring/semimodule generality already present in `Comodule`, with any ring/module-only API derived separately if needed.
